title: Technical Scout Skills — Market Regime & Trend Analysis
description: Knowledge base for the Technical Scout: market regime detection, trend analysis, technical indicators. Eyes of the firm — sees the market, doesn't trade it.

trending.definition: Price making directional moves with momentum. ADX > 25 confirms trend strength. Higher highs + higher lows = uptrend. Lower highs + lower lows = downtrend.
trending.options_implication: Trending market favors Iron Butterfly (sell ATM, wings protect against moderate trend). Strong trend (ADX>35) favors directional Credit Spreads.
trending.detection: ADX ≥ 25 + SuperTrend direction confirmed + EMA5/20 aligned

sideways.definition: Price oscillating in a range without directional momentum. ADX < 25. Equal highs and lows forming a channel.
sideways.options_implication: Sideways market is ideal for Iron Condor (sell OTM strikes, wider profit zone). Theta decay works best when spot stays in range.

choppy.definition: High volatility but no direction. Large daily ranges but no sustained trend. VIX > 20, ADX fluttering 20-30.
choppy.options_implication: Risky for any strategy. Wings must be wider. Consider sitting out or reducing lot size.
choppy.detection: VIX > 20 + ADX 20-30 + no sustained SuperTrend direction for >5 bars

thresholds.0-20: No trend / sideways — options sellers safe
thresholds.20-25: Weak trend developing — be cautious, wait for confirmation
thresholds.25-35: Trending — Iron Butterfly viable, wings at 200-300
thresholds.35-50: Strong trend — Credit Spreads preferred, wider wings
thresholds.50+: Extreme trend — reduce position size, use 350+ wings

supertrend.name: SuperTrend Indicator
supertrend.creator: Olivier Seban
values: BULLISH: price above SuperTrend line = uptrend
BEARISH: price below SuperTrend line = downtrend
NEUTRAL: price oscillating around = no clear direction
supertrend.formula: Upper Band = (High + Low)/2 + Multiplier × ATR(10). Lower Band = (High + Low)/2 - Multiplier × ATR(10). Direction flips when price closes beyond band.
supertrend.use: Primary direction indicator. Combine with ADX: Bearish ST + ADX>25 = strong downtrend → sell calls (call credit spread). Bullish ST + ADX>25 = strong uptrend → sell puts (put credit spread).

rsi.name: Relative Strength Index
rsi.creator: J. Welles Wilder

thresholds.70+: Overbought — expect pullback. Iron Fly favorable (reversion trade).
thresholds.30-: Oversold — expect bounce. Iron Fly favorable if SuperTrend is bullish.
thresholds.40-60: Neutral zone — no edge from RSI alone.

rsi.warning: In strong trends, RSI can stay overbought/oversold for extended periods. Always confirm with ADX.
rsi.formula: RSI = 100 - (100 / (1 + RS)) where RS = avg_gain / avg_loss over 14 periods

atr.name: Average True Range
atr.use: Measures volatility magnitude. ATR × 1.5 ≈ expected daily range. Helps calibrate wing width: wings must exceed daily range.

vwap.name: Volume-Weighted Average Price
vwap.use: Institutional benchmark. Spot above VWAP = intraday bullish. Spot below VWAP = intraday bearish. Trade WITH VWAP direction.
vwap.formula: VWAP = Σ(Price × Volume) / Σ(Volume) — cumulative from session open

ema_crossover.name: EMA 5/20 Crossover
ema_crossover.use: Short-term momentum. EMA5 > EMA20 = bullish momentum. EMA5 < EMA20 = bearish momentum. Alignment with SuperTrend = stronger conviction.

vix_interpretation.definition: India VIX — NIFTY's 30-day implied volatility. The 'fear index.' Derived from NIFTY option prices using Black-Scholes.

levels.0-12: Complacency — options are cheap. Iron Butterfly works but premiums are thin. Risk: VIX spike kills position.
levels.12-16: Normal — ideal for premium selling. Decent premium, manageable risk. Standard wing widths apply.
levels.16-20: Elevated — good premium but wider wings needed. Monitor for event risk.
levels.20-25: High — VIX crushing after entry = vega profit. Wider wings (300+) mandatory. Consider credit spreads.
levels.25+: Extreme — event unfolding. Reduce size or sit out. If trading, use 400+ wings and half lots.

vix_interpretation.daily_range_estimate: Expected daily move (points) = VIX% × NIFTY_spot ÷ √252. At VIX=15, spot=24500: expected daily move ≈ ₹115 points.

decision_framework.step_1: Read ADX from DuckDB. Is it >25? YES → trending. NO → sideways.
decision_framework.step_2: Read SuperTrend direction. BULLISH? BEARISH? NEUTRAL?
decision_framework.step_3: Check VIX level. Is it in a favorable range for premium selling?
decision_framework.step_4: Combine into regime: TRENDING_BULL / TRENDING_BEAR / SIDEWAYS / CHOPPY.
decision_framework.step_5: Report suitable strategies: TRENDING → Iron Fly + Credit Spreads. SIDEWAYS → Iron Condor. CHOPPY → Sit out or half size.
decision_framework.step_6: NEVER fabricate data. If DuckDB is stale or unavailable, say 'Unable to read market data — DuckDB stream may be down.'

edge_cases.adx_high_vix_low: ADX>35 but VIX<12. Strong trend with cheap options. Unusual — possible data issue. Report both readings and let Analyst decide.
edge_cases.adx_low_vix_high: ADX<20 but VIX>25. Event-driven volatility without trend. CHOPPY regime. Recommend sitting out.
edge_cases.supertrend_flat: SuperTrend flipping frequently (5+ flips in last hour). Market is undecided. Report as 'unstable' regime — caution.
edge_cases.duckdb_stale: If last DuckDB row is >5 minutes old, data feed may be down. Report: 'DuckDB data stale (last row: X seconds ago). Reading may not reflect current market.'
edge_cases.gap_open: NIFTY opens with >1% gap. Initial ADX/SuperTrend readings may be unreliable. Wait 15 minutes for indicators to settle.
